english: Comp. yon-mčód (yon mchod) 1. = yón-bdag (yon bdag) Glr. 2 for yón-bdag (yon bdag) dań (dang) mčód-gnas (mchod gnas) Mil. dispenser (of gifts) and priest. — yón-bdag (yon bdag) vulgo and in more recent literature for the sbyiń-bdag (sbyin bdag) of earlier writings, dispenser of gifts, entertainer, host, in point of fact identic with house-owner, citizen, farmer, and also at the present time used in that sense without any religious bearing; it is also the title generally used by mendicant friars in their addresses, something like 'your honour'. — yón-gnas (yon gnas) the receiver of a gift Cs.

(opp. to skyoń (skyon)) 1. good quality, excellence, valuable properties, e.g. the medicinal virtues of plants; also acquirements, accomplishments, attainments, yón-tan slób-pa (yon tan slob pa) to learn something useful Pth. and vulgo; °di bui yón-tan yin ('di bu'i yon tan yin) Dzl. for that you are indebted to the boy, this is the boy's merit; property, quality, in gen., e.g. the different tastes and effects of medicines Med.; also mystic or fantastic properties Glr. — bdag blus kyań yón-tan med (bdag blus kyang yon tan med) Glr., even if one would ransom me, it would be to no purpose, not worth while; °dód(-pai) yón(-tan) ('dod (-pa'i) yon (-tan)) v. °dód-pa ('dod pa); pán-yon (phan yon) v. pán-pa (phan pa). — 2. num.: 3.

stirrup Cs.; yob-góń (yob gong) instep of the foot Cs.; yob-lčágs (yob lcags) 'the iron of the stirrup' Cs.; yob-čéń (yob chen) = yob (yob) Cs.; yob-tág (yob thag) stirrup-leather Cs., yob-mtíl (yob mthil) the footing, yob-lúń (yob lung) (Sch. yob-lóń (yob long)) the hoop of the stirrup.
Cs. vb., adj. sbst., to swing, totter, tremble, to be unsteady; swinging etc., the swinging etc.; yóm-po (yom po), adj., yom-yóm (yom yom) Pth., yóm-me-ba (yom me ba) Mil. id.

1. dull, heavy, blunt Cs.; Tar.: yór - yor - ba (yor yor ba); but the expressions tóm-yór (thom yor) shaking, tottering, trembling, like an old man Mil., and mig-yór (mig yor) mirage, seem to indicate that the proper signification is trembling. — 2. oblique, slanting, C.
earthen-ware, crockery Schr., Cs., dkar-yól (dkar yol) china-ware, porcelain, frq.; yol-gór (yol gor) cup, bowl, Sch.

I. sbst. curtain, yól-bas °bré-ba (yol bas 'bre ba) Glr. to stretch a curtain over; yól-ba ten-pa (yol ba ten pa) Glr. to draw a curtain; yól-ba γčod-pa (yol ba gcod pa) to close the curtain (of a door), yól-ba °byéd-pa (yol ba 'byed pa) to open it Cs.; dar-yól (dar yol) silk-curtain, ras-yól (ras yol) calico-curtain; sgo-yól (sgo yol) curtain before a door. — II. vb. 1. to be past, nyi-ma-pyed yol (nyi ma phyed yol) mid-day is past, it is after-noon (about 2 o'cl.) Wdk. (v. nyiń-żág (nyin zhag)); srod yol soń (srod yol song) the evening-twilight is gone, it is complete night (about 11 o'cl.) C.; nyi-ma yól-la kad (nyi ma yol la khad) day is almost over, evening is drawing on, Dzl. 72, 6; dús-las yól-ba (dus las yol ba) to be past, both impers., it is past, it is over, and pers., he is past his prime, old, decrepit Dzl.; rluń dań čar dús-las mi yól-bas (rlung dang char dus las mi yol bas) wind and rain setting in and ceasing at the proper time Dzl. — 2. also γyól-ba (g.yol ba) C., dbyól-ba (dbyol ba), °byól-ba ('byol ba) to evade, shun, to go not to a place, mig yól-ba (mig yol ba) to look away; *lé-yol čém-po yin* (le yol chem po yin) he is very shy of work, averse to labour C.

1. slightly roasted corn, mostly barley or wheat, which on account of its transportability is generally taken by travellers along with them, as their fare on the road; fresh prepared it is much relished by the people; °brás-yos ('bras yos) rice, thus prepared S.g. — 2. hare, but only as an astronomical term, yós-lo (yos lo) the hare-year.

the yak, Bos grunniens (reckoned by the Hindu among the antilopes), fem. v. °bri-mo ('bri mo); po-γyág (pho g.yag) male yak; pa-γyág (pha g.yag) uncastrated yak-bull; γyag-rú (g.yag ru) horn of a yak, also n. of a plant, Morina Ld.; γyag-rog-żol-čéń (g.yag rog zhol chen) a very long-haired, shaggy yak Sch.

1. synon. dpal (dpal), happiness, blessing, prosperity, γyań čágs (g.yang chags) blessing comes (from), grows (out of), nif. Mil.; šor (shor) it departs, it is gone; γyań-skyób (g.yang skyob), γyań-°gúgs (g.yang 'gugs) Schl. 263, *yań-kúg* (yang khug) W. a calling forth of blessing, sacrifices and other ceremonies performed, in order to secure happiness and prosperity. — γyań-skár (g.yang skar) propitious stars or aspects; the lunar mansions no. 2
